mission: Fix the exact support-branch compression target that any future neutral source carrier would have to satisfy to reproduce the already-fixed baryogenesis freeze-out target.

conditional_hypothesis.hypothesis: A future neutral source carrier inherits the support-branch count law N_s and its characteristic source energy is the relativistic Fermi compression scale on the support branch.

theorem_1_general_crossing_law.statement: Under the compression hypothesis, matching the already-fixed baryogenesis target T_f,target determines a unique crossing radius for each source count N_s:

theorem_2_full_count_slice.statement: If the future neutral source carrier inherits the full puncture count, then the baryogenesis-scale compression crossing occurs at the unique radius R_eta reported here, far above the bounce seam.

theorem_3_seam_identification_nogo.statement: Under the same compression hypothesis, the bounce seam cannot be identified with the baryogenesis-scale source point. The compression energy at the bounce overshoots T_f,target enormously, so any successful source theorem would have to select the earlier crossing radius rather than the seam.

final_boundary.safe_statement: This theorem does not derive the missing neutral source carrier. It fixes the exact support-branch crossing law that any such carrier would have to satisfy if its scale comes from relativistic compression.
